# Amsterdam city guide

## Overview

Amsterdam is a city of canals, bicycles, and unapologetic freedom. World-class museums sit alongside brown cafes, flower markets, and neighborhoods that each have their own distinct character. It's compact, bikeable, and beautiful in a rain-soaked, golden-light way that makes you want to stay forever.

**Best time to visit**: Apr-May for tulip season and King's Day, Jun-Aug for long summer days and outdoor festivals.
**Currency**: Euro (EUR). 1 EUR ≈ 1.08 USD.
**Language tip**: Nearly everyone speaks fluent English. Dutch is appreciated but not expected. "Dank je" (thank you) is a nice touch.

## Neighborhoods

| Neighborhood | Vibe | Best for | Transit |
|---|---|---|---|
| Jordaan | Charming, artsy | Galleries, brown cafes, canal walks, markets | Tram 13/17, Westermarkt |
| De Pijp | Trendy, multicultural | Albert Cuyp Market, brunch, Heineken Experience | Tram 16/24, Albert Cuypstraat |
| Centrum/Dam Square | Touristy, central | Royal Palace, shopping, Anne Frank House | Tram, Amsterdam Centraal |
| Oud-West | Local, hip | Foodhallen, Vondelpark, boutique shops | Tram 1/11, Overtoom |
| Nieuwmarkt | Historic, lively | Chinese food, Waag building, night markets | Metro, Nieuwmarkt Station |
| NDSM Wharf | Industrial, creative | Street art, festivals, waterfront dining | Free ferry from Centraal |
| Museumplein | Cultural, green | Rijksmuseum, Van Gogh Museum, Concertgebouw | Tram 2/5, Museumplein |
| Oost (East) | Diverse, emerging | Oosterpark, Tropenmuseum, authentic eats | Tram 1/3, Oosterpark |

## Food

| Name | Category | Neighborhood | Budget | Description | Must-order |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Foodhallen | Food hall | Oud-West | $$ | Amsterdam's indoor street food market in a former tram depot. Over 20 stalls, from bao buns to bitterballen. | Bitterballen, banh mi, craft beer |
| Albert Cuyp Market | Street market | De Pijp | $ | The largest outdoor market in Amsterdam. Stroopwafels made fresh, herring stands, Surinamese roti. | Fresh stroopwafel, raw herring, Surinamese roti |
| The Pancake Bakery | Dutch | Jordaan | $$ | Pancakes the size of manhole covers with every topping imaginable. In a 17th-century canal warehouse. | Bacon and cheese pancake, apple cinnamon pancake |
| Winkel 43 | Cafe | Jordaan | $ | The apple pie here is legendary. Thick, chunky, served warm with whipped cream. Go on Noordermarkt Saturday. | Apple pie with whipped cream |
| Rijsel | French-Belgian | Oost | $$ | No menu, just a daily-changing set meal. The fried chicken and frites nights are cult favorites. Book ahead. | Fried chicken, frites, daily set menu |
| Pluk | Cafe/brunch | Centrum | $$ | Pretty-in-pink brunch spot with açai bowls, avocado toast, and fresh juices. Instagram-ready but genuinely good. | Acai bowl, avocado toast, matcha latte |
| Moeders | Dutch | Jordaan | $$ | "Mothers" restaurant with home-style Dutch food and walls covered in photos of everyone's mom. | Stamppot (mashed potato), hutspot, Dutch pea soup |
| Van Stapele Koekmakerij | Bakery | Centrum | $ | One product: a dark chocolate cookie with white chocolate center, served warm. There's always a line. | Chocolate cookie (the only item) |
| Bar Spek | Brunch | De Pijp | $$ | Excellent all-day brunch in the heart of De Pijp. Eggs every way, strong coffee, relaxed vibe. | Eggs royale, French toast, flat white |
| Ciel Bleu | Fine dining | De Pijp | $$$ | Two Michelin stars on the 23rd floor of Hotel Okura. French-Japanese fusion with city views. | Tasting menu with wine pairing |

## Landmarks and activities

| Name | Category | Neighborhood | Budget | Time needed | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Anne Frank House | History | Jordaan | $$ | 1.5 hours | The actual hiding place. Deeply moving, incredibly well-preserved. Book timed tickets exactly 6 weeks ahead. They sell out instantly. |
| Rijksmuseum | Art | Museumplein | $$ | 3-4 hours | Rembrandt's Night Watch, Vermeer's Milkmaid, and thousands more. The building itself is a masterpiece. |
| Van Gogh Museum | Art | Museumplein | $$ | 2-3 hours | The world's largest Van Gogh collection. Chronological journey through his life. Book timed entry. |
| Canal Cruise | Scenic | Central | $$ | 1 hour | See the city from the water. Evening cruises with wine are romantic. Open boats are best in good weather. |
| Vondelpark | Nature | Oud-Zuid | Free | 1-2 hours | Amsterdam's Central Park. Locals picnic, jog, and sit at the cafe terraces. Free summer concerts. |
| Jordaan Canal Walk | Walk | Jordaan | Free | 2 hours | The most photogenic canals in the city. Prinsengracht, Keizersgracht, Herengracht. Golden hour is magical. |
| NDSM Wharf | Culture | Noord | Free | 2-3 hours | Former shipyard turned creative village. Giant street art, festivals, IJ Brewery, and a vibe you won't find anywhere else. |
| Heineken Experience | Culture | De Pijp | $$ | 1.5 hours | Interactive tour of the original Heineken brewery. Ends with beer tasting. More fun than you'd expect. |
| A'DAM Lookout and Swing | Views | Noord | $$ | 1 hour | Panoramic views from the tower across the IJ river. The rooftop swing over the edge is for the brave. |
| Begijnhof | History | Centrum | Free | 30 min | A hidden courtyard of medieval houses in the city center. One of Amsterdam's secret peaceful spots. |

## Nightlife

| Name | Category | Neighborhood | Budget | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Leidseplein Area | Bars/clubs | Centrum | $$-$$$ | Amsterdam's main nightlife square. Clubs, comedy shows, and live music venues clustered together. |
| Paradiso | Live music/club | Leidseplein | $$ | A former church turned legendary music venue. Incredible acoustics, eclectic bookings. |
| Brouwerij 't IJ | Brewery | Oost | $$ | Craft brewery in a windmill. Sit on the terrace, drink local IPAs, and watch the sun go down. |
| Cafe de Dokter | Brown cafe | Centrum | $$ | Amsterdam's smallest bar, open since 1798. Barely fits 12 people. Jenever (Dutch gin) is the order. |
| Shelter | Club | Noord | $$ | Underground techno club beneath the A'DAM Tower. Serious music, serious sound system, open until morning. |

## Transit tips

- **OV-chipkaart**: Rechargeable transit card for trams, buses, Metro, and trains. Buy at Centraal Station.
- **Trams**: The main way to get around. Lines 2, 5, 13, and 17 cover most tourist areas.
- **Bikes**: Amsterdam's primary transport. Rent from MacBike or Swapfiets. Ride in bike lanes, signal turns.
- **Free ferries**: Behind Centraal Station, free ferries cross to Noord (NDSM, A'DAM Tower). Run 24 hours.
- **Walking**: The canal ring is compact. You can walk from Centraal to Museumplein in 25 minutes.
- **Pro tip**: Watch for bikes. They have right of way and they will not stop for you. Stay out of bike lanes.
- **Trains**: Amsterdam Centraal connects to Schiphol Airport (15 min), Rotterdam (40 min), and The Hague (50 min).

## Useful Dutch phrases

| English | Dutch | Romanization |
|---|---|---|
| Hello | Hallo | HAH-loh |
| Thank you | Dank je wel | DAHNK yuh VEL |
| Please | Alsjeblieft | AHL-shuh-bleeft |
| Cheers! | Proost! | PROHST |
| The bill please | De rekening, alsjeblieft | duh RAY-kuh-ning AHL-shuh-bleeft |
| Delicious! | Lekker! | LEK-ker |
| Where is...? | Waar is...? | vahr is |
| Goodbye | Doei! | DOO-ee |
